transformationClasses.playerMeaning: Make the declared runtime family follow the reference-execution comparison family before geometry tuning.
transformationClasses.generationRule: For groups whose runtime family differs from canonicalComparisonPathFamily, emit pathFamily equal to canonicalComparisonPathFamily while preserving measured geometry.
transformationClasses.expectedMetricMovement: Removes path-family blockers but should not claim object-track lift by itself.
transformationClasses.whyNotBlindTuning: The class is derived from canonical path-family decisions in the reference execution description, not numeric search.

runtimeExpressibility.runtimeConsumer: spawnChallenge resolves each enemy pathFamily from motionSpecGroup.pathFamilyHint before layout.groupPathFamilies; check-challenge-motion-profile validates runtime, layout, and contract order.
runtimeExpressibility.predictionLimits: This is label and gate alignment only. It must not claim object-track score lift unless paired with a runtime-expressible movement transform.

transformationClasses.playerMeaning: Shorten the opening cross-field line so the first visible group reads like a compact bonus-stage sweep instead of an overlong drift.
transformationClasses.generationRule: For group 1, derive pathLength from the aggregate target path-length gate and preserve the canonical cross-sweep family and lower-field score window.
invariants: group 4 and group 5 baseline geometry is preserved
transformationClasses.expectedMetricMovement: Raise group 1 aggregate object-track fit without touching late groups.
transformationClasses.whyNotBlindTuning: The compression amount is computed from the group's reference target and promotion gate, not chosen as a free numeric delta.

runtimeExpressibility.runtimeConsumer: No current runtime source field consumes abstract pathLength directly.
runtimeExpressibility.predictionLimits: The trial vector can shorten pathLength mathematically, but the compiler has not emitted reference path point edits, pathScale edits, playback timing, or a consumed motion-spec control that proves the same pathLength change in browser runtime.
runtimeExpressibility.remainingCompilerGap: Map path-length compression to explicit consumed fields such as reference path geometry, pathScaleX/pathScaleY, playbackScale, or a proven motion-spec control.

transformationClasses.playerMeaning: Keep groups from lingering too long in the lower field when the reference expects an upper or transitional pass.
transformationClasses.generationRule: For groups with positive lower-field overstay, blend lowerFieldShare toward the aggregate target within the reference-execution lower-field gate.
invariants: path family remains canonical for touched groups
scoreable route baseline is preserved unless a later measured route probe says otherwise
group 4 and group 5 cannot be allowed to regress
transformationClasses.expectedMetricMovement: Reduce lower-field overstay warnings, especially group 2, without treating low-field geometry as a standalone win.
transformationClasses.whyNotBlindTuning: Groups are selected from measured lower-field deltas, and the target value comes from the reference execution description.

runtimeExpressibility.runtimeConsumer: Stage 7 motion-spec controls are consumed by the challenge path generator, but lowerFieldShare is not itself a runtime field.
runtimeExpressibility.predictionLimits: The current semantic candidate emits lowerFieldShare deltas, not lowerFieldBias/yOffset values with a browser-proven transfer function.
runtimeExpressibility.remainingCompilerGap: Derive concrete lowerFieldBias/yOffset or reference-path geometry changes and verify their actual lower-field share in browser runtime.

transformationClasses.playerMeaning: Bring group visible windows closer to the reference phase order without broad stage retiming.
transformationClasses.generationRule: For groups with visible start/end drift, derive compiled runtime controls from the target window instead of emitting visibleStartS/visibleEndS as trial-only vectors.
invariants: phase order remains group 1 through group 5
target count and no-combat challenge meaning are preserved
group 4 and group 5 baseline object-track floors are protected
transformationClasses.expectedMetricMovement: Improve timing components while exposing whether geometry worsens when phase timing is tightened.
transformationClasses.whyNotBlindTuning: Timing comes from object-track windows in the reference execution description.

generatedRuntimeControls.expectedVisibleEffect: moves the group visible window earlier or later relative to other groups
changes when the reference path enters its exit/overrun behavior
stretches or compresses reference path playback in real browser-visible time
none until the runtime consumes exitS

runtimeExpressibility.runtimeConsumer: spawnChallenge() consumes groupSpawnOffsets or motionSpecGroup.spawnOffsetS; applyReferenceChallengePath() consumes phaseDurations.trackS; the challenge update loop consumes referencePath.playbackScale. phaseDurations.exitS is declared but not currently consumed.
runtimeExpressibility.predictionLimits: The trial score can improve by assigning visible windows directly. Source readiness requires compiledRuntimeControls plus proof that the emitted controls produce browser-visible timing/path movement.
runtimeExpressibility.remainingCompilerGap: Current generated candidates must emit compiledRuntimeControls and cite the proof artifact; visibleStartS/visibleEndS alone remains analysis-only.

transformationClasses.playerMeaning: Reject motion ideas that improve object-track math by collapsing playable bonus lanes.
transformationClasses.generationRule: Attach baseline scoreable-route evidence as an invariant guardrail unless a candidate supplies stronger measured route evidence.
invariants: active score windows do not regress
multi-target lane share does not regress
center-lane bias remains below the trial floor
transformationClasses.expectedMetricMovement: No direct object-track lift; protects player-facing route quality.
transformationClasses.whyNotBlindTuning: The class preserves measured route evidence as a semantic guardrail, not a tunable geometry parameter.

runtimeExpressibility.runtimeConsumer: No runtime source field changes; this class carries shot-opportunity evidence as an invariant.
runtimeExpressibility.predictionLimits: Cannot make a candidate source-ready by itself.

transformationClasses.playerMeaning: Treat late Challenge 2 groups as hard readability/identity guardrails while improving the opener.
transformationClasses.generationRule: Carry group 4 and group 5 baseline vectors into the candidate and mark them as protected invariants unless a measured candidate explicitly improves them.
invariants: group 4 aggregate score stays at or above baseline
group 5 aggregate score stays at or above baseline
late boss-led cleanup does not become the casualty of group 1 tuning
transformationClasses.expectedMetricMovement: Prevents false positive total-score movement that hides late-group regression.
transformationClasses.whyNotBlindTuning: The class encodes the prior rejected-candidate lesson as an explicit semantic guardrail.

runtimeExpressibility.runtimeConsumer: No runtime source field changes unless another transform touches groups 4 or 5; this class preserves measured group floors.
runtimeExpressibility.predictionLimits: A predictedRuntimeVector used only for protection is not proof that a source edit can express a new vector.

promotionPolicy.runtimeSourceCandidateOnly: A semantic batch winner may authorize exactly one runtime source candidate, not a runtime keeper.
